title: Afzelia martabanica
taxonomy: Afzelia martabanica is a species of tree in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is native to Southeast Asia. The species was first described by Joseph Dalton Hooker in 1875. Afzelia martabanica is a member of the genus Afzelia, which comprises around 50 species of trees. The tree is often found in tropical forests and woodlands in Southeast Asia.
uses: Afzelia martabanica has been traditionally used in various medicinal applications. Its bark is used to treat fever and other diseases. In traditional medicine, the plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. The stem and leaves of Afzelia martabanica are used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems. Further research is required to confirm the medicinal uses of this plant.
benefits: Afzelia martabanica has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, where its extracts are believed to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The plant's extracts may help in protecting against various diseases, including those caused by bacterial and fungal infections. Its antioxidant properties may also contribute to the reduction of oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. Furthermore, the plant has been studied for its potential anticancer effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Afzelia martabanica.

Additionally, Afzelia martabanica is believed to possess antioxidant properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. parts: Afzelia martabanica is a species of tree that has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of Asia. The medicinal parts of Afzelia martabanica include its bark, leaves, and seeds, which have been used to treat a range of ailments. The bark is used to treat fever and rheumatism, while the leaves are used to treat digestive issues and the seeds are used to treat various skin conditions. The tree's roots are also believed to have medicinal properties, although they are less commonly used. The specific uses of Afzelia martabanica's medicinal parts can vary depending on the region and the traditional medicine practice.
